member.name: T:Autofac.Pooling.AutofacPooledObjectPolicy`1
member.summary: Provides the needed for creating/returning objects in the pool in an Autofac way.
member.typeparam: The type of object being pooled.

member.name: M:Autofac.Pooling.AutofacPooledObjectPolicy`1.#ctor(Autofac.Core.Service,Autofac.ILifetimeScope,Autofac.Pooling.IPooledRegistrationPolicy{`0})
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The pooled instance service used to resolve a new instance of the pooled items.
The owning scope of the pool.
The registration policy for the pool.

member.name: T:Autofac.Pooling.DefaultPooledRegistrationPolicy`1
member.summary: Provides the default .
member.typeparam: The type of object being pooled.

member.name: M:Autofac.Pooling.DefaultPooledRegistrationPolicy`1.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: M:Autofac.Pooling.DefaultPooledRegistrationPolicy`1.#ctor(System.Int32)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The maximum number of instances that should be retained in the pool.

member.name: T:Autofac.Pooling.IPooledComponent
member.summary: Components implementing this interface are aware that they are pooled, so will be notified when they are retrieved from the pool, and when they are returned to the pool.

member.name: M:Autofac.Pooling.IPooledComponent.OnGetFromPool(Autofac.IComponentContext,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Autofac.Core.Parameter})
member.summary: Invoked when this instance is retrieved from the pool. Any dependencies retrieved using the provided MUST be discarded in , otherwise memory leaks may occur.
member.param: The component context for the current resolve request.
The parameters to the resolve request.

summary.para: Invoked when this instance is being returned to the pool. Any dependencies retrieved in should be discarded in this method.

If the maximum pool capacity has been reached (or a custom decides not to return the instance to the pool), this method will be invoked, but then the object will not be put back in the pool (and may be disposed if the component implements ).

member.name: T:Autofac.Pooling.IPooledRegistrationPolicy`1
member.summary: Defines the interface for a custom pooled registration policy, that defines what happens when an instance is retrieved from the pool, and what happens when the instance is returned to the pool.
member.typeparam: The type of object being pooled.

member.name: P:Autofac.Pooling.IPooledRegistrationPolicy`1.MaximumRetained
member.summary: Gets a value indicating the maximum number of items that will be retained in the pool. This is not a limit on the number of objects that can be created, it's the threshold past which any objects returned to the pool will be discarded.

member.name: M:Autofac.Pooling.IPooledRegistrationPolicy`1.Get(Autofac.IComponentContext,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Autofac.Core.Parameter},System.Func{`0})
member.summary: Invoked when an instance of is requested. The policy can invoke to retrieve an instance from the pool. Equally, it could decide to ignore the pool, and just return a custom instance.
member.param: The current component context.
The set of parameters for the resolve request accessing the pool.
A callback that will retrieve an item from the underlying pool of objects.

member.name: M:Autofac.Pooling.IPooledRegistrationPolicy`1.Return(`0)
member.summary: Invoked when an object is about to be returned into the pool. This method should be used to clean up the state of the object after being used (resetting connections, releasing temporary resources, etc).
member.param: The pooled object.
member.returns: True if the object should be returned to the pool. False if it should not be placed back in the pool (and will be disposed immediately if it implements ).

member.name: T:Autofac.Pooling.PoolActivator`1
member.summary: An activator for creating new instances.
member.typeparam: The limit type of the objects in the pool.

member.name: M:Autofac.Pooling.PoolActivator`1.#ctor(Autofac.Core.Service,Autofac.Pooling.IPooledRegistrationPolicy{`0})
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The service used to resolve new instances of the pooled registration.
The pool policy.

member.name: T:Autofac.Pooling.PooledInstanceTracker`1
member.summary: Tracking class that that will return the wrapped instance to the provided pool when it is disposed.
member.typeparam: The type of the object being pooled.

member.name: M:Autofac.Pooling.PooledInstanceTracker`1.#ctor(Microsoft.Extensions.ObjectPool.ObjectPool{`0},`0)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The pool.
The instance to wrap.

member.name: P:Autofac.Pooling.PooledInstanceTracker`1.Instance
member.summary: Gets the wrapped instance.

member.name: M:Autofac.Pooling.PooledInstanceTracker`1.Dispose
member.summary: Disposes the container, returning the instance to the pool.

member.name: T:Autofac.Pooling.PooledInstanceUnpackMiddleware`1
member.summary: Middleware for extracting the requested instance from the pool instance container.
member.typeparam: The limit type of the registration that has been pooled.

member.name: T:Autofac.Pooling.PooledLifetime
member.summary: Lifetime wrapper to help us detect if we finished registering in a pooled configuration.

member.name: T:Autofac.Pooling.PoolGetActivator`1
member.summary: Activator that resolves the object pool and retrieves from that rather than creating a new instance.
member.typeparam: The limit type of the objects in the pool.

member.name: M:Autofac.Pooling.PoolGetActivator`1.#ctor(Autofac.Pooling.PoolService,Autofac.Pooling.IPooledRegistrationPolicy{`0})
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The service used to access the pool.
The registration policy for the pool.

member.name: T:Autofac.Pooling.PoolGetActivatorResources
member.summary: A strongly-typed resource class, for looking up localized strings, etc.

member.name: P:Autofac.Pooling.PoolGetActivatorResources.ResourceManager
member.summary: Returns the cached ResourceManager instance used by this class.

member.name: P:Autofac.Pooling.PoolGetActivatorResources.Culture
member.summary: Overrides the current thread's CurrentUICulture property for all resource lookups using this strongly typed resource class.

member.name: P:Autofac.Pooling.PoolGetActivatorResources.PolicyMustReturnInstance
member.summary: Looks up a localized string similar to The custom pool policy provided by {0} did not return an instance of {1} when requested..

member.name: T:Autofac.Pooling.PoolService
member.summary: Defines a service used to access an , ensuring that each pooled item registration gets a different pool.

member.name: M:Autofac.Pooling.PoolService.#ctor(Autofac.Core.IComponentRegistration)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The registration for the pooled item.

member.name: T:Autofac.Pooling.PoolServiceResources
member.summary: A strongly-typed resource class, for looking up localized strings, etc.

member.name: P:Autofac.Pooling.PoolServiceResources.ResourceManager
member.summary: Returns the cached ResourceManager instance used by this class.

member.name: P:Autofac.Pooling.PoolServiceResources.Culture
member.summary: Overrides the current thread's CurrentUICulture property for all resource lookups using this strongly typed resource class.

member.name: P:Autofac.Pooling.PoolServiceResources.Description
member.summary: Looks up a localized string similar to Pool of {0}.

member.name: T:Autofac.Pooling.RegistrationExtensions
member.summary: Provides registration methods for setting up pooled instances.

member.name: M:Autofac.Pooling.RegistrationExtensions.PooledInstancePerLifetimeScope``3(Autofac.Builder.IRegistrationBuilder{``0,``1,``2})
member.summary: Configure the component so that every dependent component or manual resolve within a single will return the same, shared instance, retrieved from a single pool of instances shared by all lifetime scopes. When the scope ends, the instance will be returned to the pool.

remarks.para: The size of the pool created with this method defaults to twice the number of processors ( x 2). If more instances are requested than the pool size, those instances may not be returned to the pool, but will instead be disposed/discarded.
If a component needs to perform behaviour when it is retrieved from or returned to the pool, it can implement , or use the overload of this method that accepts a custom .

member.param: The registration.

member.name: M:Autofac.Pooling.RegistrationExtensions.PooledInstancePerLifetimeScope``3(Autofac.Builder.IRegistrationBuilder{``0,``1,``2},System.Int32)
member.summary: Configure the component so that every dependent component or manual resolve within a single will return the same, shared instance, retrieved from a single pool of instances shared by all lifetime scopes. When the scope ends, the instance will be returned to the pool.

remarks.para: The size of the pool created with this method is equal to . If more instances are requested than the pool size, those instances may not be returned to the pool, but will instead be disposed/discarded.
If a component needs to perform behaviour when it is retrieved from or returned to the pool, it can implement , or use the overload of this method that accepts a custom .

member.param: The registration.
The maximum number of instances to retain in the pool.

member.name: M:Autofac.Pooling.RegistrationExtensions.PooledInstancePerLifetimeScope``3(Autofac.Builder.IRegistrationBuilder{``0,``1,``2},Autofac.Pooling.IPooledRegistrationPolicy{``0})
member.summary: Configure the component so that every dependent component or manual resolve within a single will return the same, shared instance, retrieved from a single pool of instances shared by all lifetime scopes. When the scope ends, the instance will be returned to the pool.

remarks.para: This method accepts a custom that provides fine-grained control of the retrieval of instances from the pool, and allows the implementer to choose whether or not the instance should even be returned to the pool.

The size of the pool created with this method is equal to the value on the . If more instances are requested than the pool size, those instances may not be returned to the pool, but will instead be disposed/discarded.

member.param: The registration.
A custom policy for controlling pool behaviour.

member.name: M:Autofac.Pooling.RegistrationExtensions.PooledInstancePerMatchingLifetimeScope``3(Autofac.Builder.IRegistrationBuilder{``0,``1,``2},System.Object[])
member.summary: Configure the component so that every dependent component or manual resolve within a tagged with any of the provided tags value gets the same, shared instance, retrieved from a single pool of instances shared by all lifetime scopes. When the scope ends, the instance will be returned to the pool. Dependent components in lifetime scopes that are children of the tagged scope will share the parent's instance. If no appropriately tagged scope can be found in the hierarchy an is thrown.

remarks.para: The size of the pool created with this method defaults to twice the number of processors ( x 2). If more instances are requested than the pool size, those instances may not be returned to the pool, but will instead be disposed/discarded.
If a component needs to perform behaviour when it is retrieved from or returned to the pool, it can implement , or use the overload of this method that accepts a custom .

member.param: The registration.
Tags applied to matching lifetime scopes.

member.name: M:Autofac.Pooling.RegistrationExtensions.PooledInstancePerMatchingLifetimeScope``3(Autofac.Builder.IRegistrationBuilder{``0,``1,``2},System.Int32,System.Object[])
member.summary: Configure the component so that every dependent component or manual resolve within a tagged with any of the provided tags value gets the same, shared instance, retrieved from a single pool of instances shared by all lifetime scopes. When the scope ends, the instance will be returned to the pool. Dependent components in lifetime scopes that are children of the tagged scope will share the parent's instance. If no appropriately tagged scope can be found in the hierarchy an is thrown.

remarks.para: The size of the pool created with this method is equal to . If more instances are requested than the pool size, those instances may not be returned to the pool, but will instead be disposed/discarded.
If a component needs to perform behaviour when it is retrieved from or returned to the pool, it can implement , or use the overload of this method that accepts a custom .

member.param: The registration.
The maximum number of instances to retain in the pool.
Tags applied to matching lifetime scopes.

member.name: M:Autofac.Pooling.RegistrationExtensions.PooledInstancePerMatchingLifetimeScope``3(Autofac.Builder.IRegistrationBuilder{``0,``1,``2},Autofac.Pooling.IPooledRegistrationPolicy{``0},System.Object[])
member.summary: Configure the component so that every dependent component or manual resolve within a tagged with any of the provided tags value gets the same, shared instance, retrieved from a single pool of instances shared by all lifetime scopes. When the scope ends, the instance will be returned to the pool. Dependent components in lifetime scopes that are children of the tagged scope will share the parent's instance. If no appropriately tagged scope can be found in the hierarchy an is thrown.

remarks.para: This method accepts a custom that provides fine-grained control of the retrieval of instances from the pool, and allows the implementer to choose whether or not the instance should even be returned to the pool.

The size of the pool created with this method is equal to the value on the . If more instances are requested than the pool size, those instances may not be returned to the pool, but will instead be disposed/discarded.

member.param: The registration.
A custom policy for controlling pool behaviour.
Tags applied to matching lifetime scopes.

member.name: T:Autofac.Pooling.RegistrationExtensionsResources
member.summary: A strongly-typed resource class, for looking up localized strings, etc.

member.name: P:Autofac.Pooling.RegistrationExtensionsResources.ResourceManager
member.summary: Returns the cached ResourceManager instance used by this class.

member.name: P:Autofac.Pooling.RegistrationExtensionsResources.Culture
member.summary: Overrides the current thread's CurrentUICulture property for all resource lookups using this strongly typed resource class.

member.name: P:Autofac.Pooling.RegistrationExtensionsResources.CannotUseProvidedInstances
member.summary: Looks up a localized string similar to Pooled registrations cannot be used with provided instances. Use a typed registration or a delegate registration instead..

member.name: P:Autofac.Pooling.RegistrationExtensionsResources.OnReleaseNotSupported
member.summary: Looks up a localized string similar to It is not possible to use custom OnRelease events for pooled registrations. Instead, either provide a custom IPooledRegistrationPolicy to handle items being returned to pool, or implement IPooledComponent on the component's type..

member.name: P:Autofac.Pooling.RegistrationExtensionsResources.RequiresCallbackContainer
member.summary: Looks up a localized string similar to You can only specified a pooled registration for registrations that have a callback container attached (e.g., one that was made with a standard ContainerBuilder extension method)..
